MENO

FACTS 
QUESTION 
Al.~SWER 
October 28, 1988

. 
Bruce Robeck ,~~ 
Telephone advice to Ronald Sohigian, Superior Court Judge 
Section 87200(a) (2) , 82020. 
A newly appointed superior court judge was sworn-in to 
office on October 1st. Prior to assuming office, the judge 
had made political contributions from personal funds but 
did not have any filing obigations because of the contri-
bution activity. He has not made any contributions nor 
has he received any contributions since he was svJOrn-in. 
What is the campaign disclosure obligation of a newly 
appointed judge and when does the filing obligation begin? 
A newly appointed judge incurs a campaign disclosure 
filing obligation: 
(1) 
from the date of swearing-in through the remainder 
of the calendar year (no activity prior to being 
sworn-in would trigger a new filing obligation as 
as a judge; 
(2) 
any contributions or expenditures after being 
sworn-in to office may create a filing obligation 
for the judge (a 470 or a 490). 
Note: 
SB 2798, Ch. 708, Stats. 1988 changes the filing 
obligation of a judge who contributes less than $1 000 
from personal ftmds.

sections.question_synthetic: A newly appointed superior court judge seeks clarification on when campaign disclosure filing obligations commence and what activities trigger such obligations.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Campaign disclosure obligations for a newly appointed judge begin from the date of swearing-in and apply to contributions or expenditures made after assuming office, not to any activity prior to taking office.

Question and Answer sections are clearly present. Reference to SB 2798 regarding contributions under $1,000 is included. High confidence extraction despite some OCR artifacts in header.

Prior to assuming office, the judge had made political contributions from personal funds but did not have any filing obigations because of the contri- bution activity. He has not made any contributions nor has he received any contributions since he was svJOrn-in. What is the campaign disclosure obligation of a newly appointed judge and when does the filing obligation begin? A newly appointed judge incurs a campaign disclosure filing obligation: (1) from the date of swearing-in through the remainder of the calendar year (no activity prior to being sworn-in would trigger a new filing obligation as as a judge; (2) any contributions or expenditures after being sworn-in to office may create a filing obligation for the judge (a 470 or a 490). embedding.summary: This FPPC advice letter addresses the campaign disclosure filing obligations of a newly appointed superior court judge who was sworn in on October 1, 1988. The FPPC advises that filing obligations commence from the swearing-in date and apply only to contributions and expenditures made after assuming office.
